jobs.title: Cardiovascular Program Coordinator (Part Time, Day)
jobs.description:
Job Description

At NorthBay Health the Cardiovascular Program Coordinator is a RN who provides leadership across the continuum to promote excellence in cardiovascular patient care. This person has responsibility and accountability for operational design, program implementation, policy development, and population management of cardiovascular patient (Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS), heart failure, and other cardiac or vascular diagnosis). The Cardiovascular Program Coordinator’s role is to promote the provision of effective, high quality patient care to cardiovascular patients across the continuum with a collegial and interdisciplinary focus.

The following responsibilities are included: coordination of fiscal and other resources, compliance with regulatory and professional standards, and fostering of interdisciplinary, collaborative relationships within the cardiology department and between other hospital departments. He/she will seek and provide educational programs for staff, physicians and community pertinent to the cardiovascular patient population served. Other responsibilities include coordination with the appropriate EMS and county agencies related to best practice responses and scene transports for patients experiencing cardiac emergencies, and coordination of quality and performance improvement initiatives.

The Cardiovascular Program Coordinator works collaboratively with the ED Chest Pain Center Medical Director, Medical Director-Cardiology, Medical Director-Interventional Lab, the Sr. Director – Critical Care & Cardiopulmonary Services, the clinical, and operational team to ensure that all patient care activities related to patients with cardiac-related conditions are standardized and based on current best practices. The Cardiovascular Program Coordinator ensures continuous departmental and organizational compliance with accreditation standards as established by the ACC and other applicable accrediting and regulatory bodies. The Cardiovascular Program Coordinator is responsible for data collection and analysis activities surrounding the cardiac and vascular programs, and other cardiovascular focused performance improvement activities.

Qualifications

Education:

Graduate from an accredited school of registered nursing.
Bachelor’s Degree in Nursing or related health care field is required.
Master’s Degree in Nursing or related health care field is required or required to be in progress. One of the degrees must be in nursing.

Licensure

Current California RN Licensure.
Certification by the American Nurse Credentialing Center preferred.
Current BLS and ACLS certification required.

Experience

Minimum 3 years of experience involved in the direct care of the cardiac patient in either the ED, Cath Lab, Cardiology, CCU or Telemetry.
Working knowledge of population management preferred.
Previous experience in an accredited chest pain center preferred.

Skills

Excellent oral and written communication skills required.
Must possess high professional standards.
Must be well organized, be able to set priorities, work independently and is an effective team member.
Word Processing skills required.
Statistical/data management skills preferred.

About Us

Interpersonal Skills and Values

Demonstrates NorthBay Health’s True North Values: Nurture Care, Own It, Respect Relationships, Build Trust, and Hardwire Excellence. These values guide behavior, accountability, teamwork, and commitment to high-quality patient care.

Why NorthBay Health

NorthBay Health is an independent, nonprofit health system serving the Napa, Solano, and Yolo County regions. We are expanding access to care across our communities through two acute-care hospitals, including a Level II Trauma Center and a Level III NICU maternity unit, along with a cancer center, urgent care locations, and a growing network of primary and specialty care clinics.

We provide advanced services in cardiovascular care, neuroscience, orthopedics, surgery, and outpatient specialties. NorthBay Health is nationally recognized for quality care, including Magnet with Distinction designation for nursing and multiple U.S. News and World Report high performing recognitions.

We are committed to being the trusted healthcare partner of choice and offer an environment where employees can grow, contribute meaningfully, and support the health of our communities.

NorthBay Health Benefits Options

NorthBay Health offers a comprehensive benefits package based on established eligibility requirements. Benefits may include medical, dental, and vision insurance, life, disability, and long-term care coverage, paid time off including vacation, sick leave, holidays, and bereavement, a 403(b) retirement plan with employer match, education reimbursement for eligible roles, professional development and training programs, Employee Assistance Program, wellness programs, recognition programs, shift differentials, and market-based compensation review and increases subject to approval and organizational performance.

Compensation Structure

NorthBay Health uses a structured compensation framework. Staff-level positions use a step-based system (Steps 1–5) based on years of directly related experience, with Step 5 representing 20 or more years of experience in the role. Manager level and above positions are paid a fixed annual base salary and are eligible for a variable incentive compensation plan. Physician compensation is structured based on specialty and role requirements.

Remote Work Disclosure

NorthBay Health is primarily an onsite organization due to the nature of healthcare. Some roles may allow hybrid or remote work based on business needs.

Remote work is not supported in Washington, Ohio, Wyoming, North Dakota, Puerto Rico, the U.S. Virgin Islands, or outside of the United States.

Notice to Recruitment Agencies

NorthBay Health utilizes a managed service provider (MSP) for agency partnerships and is not currently engaging external recruiting firms outside of established agreements. We do not accept unsolicited resumes or third-party candidate submissions. Please do not contact NorthBay Health employees, leaders, physicians, or hiring managers regarding recruitment or job postings.

More Information

Visit NorthBay Health Careers for recruitment FAQs and additional information.

jobs.title: GIS Technician
jobs.description:
Job Specifications

Black Hills Energy is people powered and purpose driven. Our team uses the power of energy to improve life for over one million customers in 800+ communities across the West and Midwest. We seek talented, caring people who embody our core values and contribute to a culture of inclusion and growth. As an organization, we believe the best part of working on our team is our commitment to making tomorrow better than today—for our customers, communities and each other.

Position Summary

The GIS Technician's responsibilities include as-built interpretation, as-built posting to GIS, maintenance of the GIS database, mobile data collection support, communication with field operations and engineers, map production, occasional CAD work, and other miscellaneous projects as directed by the GIS Supervisor.

Pay Range:
$23.39 - $35.24 per hour (This posting includes the full pay range for this position. Pay is based on a number of factors and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and internal equity.)

Reporting Relationship:
GIS Supervisor

Location:
Fayetteville, Arkansas or Council Bluffs, Iowa

Essential Functions

Maintain the Esri Gas GIS database as well as quality check the data for inaccuracies.
Interpret gas transmission and distribution as-built drawings and post to GIS.
Understand Gas GIS operations and engineering processes as well as the gas assets and their functions.
Support the Digital As-built program. This means managing and support the system as well as training and support its usage in the field. Produce maps as required for Operations and Maintenance activities, system analysis, marketing, growth projects, etc.
Effectively communicate with staff at all levels regarding GIS.
Check accuracy of mapping system via field verification.
Diagnose software/hardware compatibility issues related to GIS products.
Use Esri tools such as ArcGIS (ArcMap & Pro) Desktop, desktop extensions (e.g. APR), ArcGIS Server, and Esri Portal in order to perform above job functions.
Provide feedback on above tools and processes in an effort to continuously improve gas as-builting accuracy, completeness, and efficiency.

Additional Responsibilities

Other duties as assigned.

What Is Required

High School Diploma or equivalent
Minimum of (1) one year of experience using Esri Desktop and Server/Portal based software

What Is Desired

Experience updating and maintaining a gas utility GIS is helpful.
Basic knowledge of Esri GIS systems and principles.
Basic knowledge of relational database management (RDBMS) concepts.
Basic knowledge of gas transmission and distribution systems.
Ability to think, work, and act independently as well as in a team environment to develop creative, analytical solutions to resolve complex problems.
Excellent customer service and interpersonal skills.
Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; ability to prepare and give presentations and trainings.
Strong organizational and time management skills.
Ability to prioritize and complete multiple tasks in a timely manner with minimal direction.
Proficiency in computer applications including Word, Excel, Power Point, Access, Outlook and various specialized software.

This description is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of responsibilities, duties, and requirements for employees in this position. Job descriptions may and do change periodically. Where positions are covered by a collective bargaining unit agreement, the terms and conditions of the agreement will apply.

About our Company:
We are a customer, growth and safety focused utility company that is dedicated to our communities. We improve life with energy as an energy partner of choice. Our diverse culture fuels unique perspectives, opening doors to new insights and possibilities. Based in Rapid City, South Dakota, we have over 3000 employees and serve 1.3 million natural gas and electric utility customers across eight states (South Dakota, Montana, Wyoming, Colorado, Nebraska, Iowa, Kansas, and Arkansas).

Enjoy our Comprehensive Benefits Package!
Annual discretionary bonuses, 401(k) (6% company match and up to 9% company retirement contribution), tuition reimbursement, generous paid time off benefits, including paid holidays and parental leave, company paid life insurance and disability benefits (short and long term), critical illness, accident & group hospital insurance, pet insurance, an employee assistance program and well-being benefits, and competitive medical, dental and vision insurance.

Candidates must successfully pass a pre-employment drug screen and background check. If there is anything that may show up in these reports that may conflict with the position requirements, feel free to contact the Black Hills Energy recruiting team at jobs@blackhillscorp.com.

Black Hills Energy does not sponsor applicants for work visas. All applicants must be legally authorized to work in the US.

We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, age, or status as a protected veteran. If you require reasonable accommodation, please visit careers.blackhillsenergy.com for more information.

jobs.title: Data Analyst
jobs.description:
At World Kinect, our employees are the key to our global success. We are industry leaders due to the innumerable talents of our approximately 5000 strong professional team. Our people thrive in an entrepreneurial and culturally-diverse environment, where innovative thinking, collaboration and efficient execution are highly valued. Our high-performance culture is what allows us to drive sustained growth. Stronger together, we promote an environment where individuals can thrive.

Data Analyst

Location:
Central and Eastern US (Remote)

Visa Sponsorship:
At this time, we are not considering candidates who require immediate or future sponsorship for this position.

About The Role

The Data Analyst at World Fuel
will help help drive business value by leveraging data, analytics, and technology to optimize commercial and operational performance. This role partners closely with pricing, sales, supply, and trading teams to improve revenue management, unit margins, and decision‑making through data‑driven insights and automation.

You’ll work with large, complex datasets to build analytical tools, dashboards, and models that support pricing strategy, margin optimization, and business performance across the WFS Land business.

What You’ll Do

Build and maintain automated data pipelines, tools, and models to support revenue and pricing decisions
Design and enhance pricing and revenue optimization algorithms
Analyze large datasets to identify margin and revenue improvement opportunities
Develop dashboards and reports using tools such as Tableau or Power BI
Perform ad‑hoc analyses and identify opportunities to automate recurring requests
Validate and reconcile data across multiple systems to ensure accuracy
Translate analytical results into actionable insights for business partners
Support integration of acquisitions into revenue and pricing workflows
Partner with stakeholders to understand business processes and improve performance through analytics and automation

What You Bring

Education

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Management Information Systems, Economics, Engineering, Operations Research, Mathematics, Applied Mathematics, or a related quantitative field.

Minimum 2 years of relevant professional experience in analytics, data analysis, or a related quantitative role.

Technical Skills

Strong experience working with large datasets and applied analytics
Proficiency in SQL and Python
Experience with ETL tools such as Alteryx
Experience building dashboards and visualizations (Tableau, Power BI)
Comfort implementing analytical and mathematical models

Core Competencies

Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills
Ability to translate complex ideas into practical, business‑ready solutions
Clear communication skills with both technical and non‑technical audiences
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment

Preferred Experience

Knowledge of the transportation fuel distribution business (gasoline, diesel)
Experience with fuel pricing, contracts, spot pricing, or CPQ tools

Why Join World Fuel

World Fuel is a global leader in energy and fuel solutions, supporting customers across land, aviation, and marine markets. This role offers exposure to high‑impact commercial analytics, advanced data solutions, and cross‑functional collaboration within a dynamic, data‑driven organization.

#WFS

World Kinect is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, age, or any other characteristic protected by law.

World Fuel Services, Inc.

What you'll do...Role summary:

The (USA) Data Analyst II plays a critical role in supporting business decision-making through comprehensive data analysis, quality management, and visualization. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to understand business context and communicate findings effectively. Proficiency in SQL, Python, and business intelligence tools is essential to support data-driven strategies and continuous improvement initiatives within a dynamic environment.

About the team:

The Transportation - Inbound Planning and Optimization team enhances the efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and service quality of inbound transportation operations. By leveraging advanced analytics, data-driven insights, and collaboration with operational partners, the team optimizes shipment planning, routing, and execution. Responsibilities include analyzing shipment patterns, ensuring data quality, conducting root cause analysis, and developing scalable solutions. Utilizing tools such as SQL, Python, and developing Agentic AI solutions, the team drives continuous improvement, process automation, and reliable transportation planning aligned with organizational goals. This team plays a vital role in translating business needs into actionable projects and system enhancements.

Identify, extract, and validate data from multiple sources to support business requirements and process optimization.
Perform initial and ongoing data quality checks, ensuring data is fit for purpose and meets business needs.
Apply data strategy principles, including data governance, accessibility, and scalability, to routine and complex business problems.
Conduct exploratory data analysis using SQL, Python, and statistical techniques to uncover trends, outliers, and opportunities for improvement.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business context, develop business cases, and translate requirements into actionable projects.
Demonstrate strong project management skills, stakeholder management, and a continuous improvement mindset.

What you'll bring:

Proficiency in SQL, Python, and data visualization tools such as Tableau to analyze and present complex data effectively.
Strong understanding of data quality management, including profiling, cleansing, and validation techniques.
Experience in exploratory data analysis to identify trends, correlations, and outliers.
Ability to interpret business context across multiple domains and translate data insights into actionable recommendations.
Skilled in communicating findings clearly to stakeholders and supporting decision-making processes.
Proficient with relational databases, business intelligence tools (Tableau preferred)
Demonstrated project management capabilities and experience with ad hoc reporting.
Critical thinking, curiosity, and a problem-solving mindset.

What you'll do...Role summary:

As a Senior Data Analyst at Walmart, you will leverage advanced data management and analytical expertise to identify, assess, and interpret complex business data across multiple domains. You will translate business requirements into actionable insights, applying rigorous data quality standards and visualization techniques to support strategic decision-making. Collaborating with cross-functional teams, you will drive data-driven solutions that enhance operational efficiency and business outcomes. This role demands a strong understanding of data ecosystems, statistical methods, and effective communication to influence stakeholders and mentor junior analysts in delivering impactful results.

About the team:

The Walmart+ Core Ops & Strategy Enablement Data team develops scalable tools that transform complex data into actionable insights. They conduct detailed analyses and design rigorous experimentation frameworks to support strategic initiatives. Collaborating with cross-functional partners, the team delivers advanced insights that reveal performance drivers beyond standard reporting. Leveraging expertise in big data, SQL, Python, and Tableau, they enable data-driven decision-making and continuous improvement. This team leads projects end-to-end, manages stakeholder relationships, and fosters a culture of learning and innovation to drive impactful results across the Walmart+ ecosystem.

What you'll do:

Bring data to life through compelling storytelling, translating complex insights into clear narratives that resonate with senior stakeholders and shape the direction of Walmart+ strategy.
Build scalable data foundations and consumption tools by transforming complex data into clean, automated pipelines and reusable assets that power self-service insights across teams.
Design intuitive dashboards and reporting experiences that go beyond descriptive metrics, enabling cross-functional teams to explore performance deeply and understand the underlying drivers and "why" behind results.
Partner closely with Acquisition, Retention, Operations, Member Care, and other cross-functional teams to deeply understand their strategic priorities and translate them into tailored insights.

What you'll bring:

A member- and customer-first mindset: you anchor every insight and recommendation in how it drives real value for Walmart+ members, keeping the customer and member perspective at the center of every decision.
A clear and persuasive ability to translate data into compelling narratives: you connect insights to strategy in a way that resonates with senior stakeholders and drives informed decision-making
A sharp eye for turning data into visual clarity: you design intuitive, self-service dashboards and tools that communicate insights effectively and empower stakeholders to explore performance with confidence.

jobs.title: Data Analyst (Consumer Product Safety), Limited Duration
jobs.description:
Who We Are

Consumer Reports is an independent, nonprofit organization dedicated to a fair and just marketplace for all. CR is known for our rigorous testing and trusted ratings on thousands of products and services. We report extensively on consumer trends and challenges, and survey millions of people in the U.S. each year. We leverage our evidence-based approach to advocate for consumer rights, working with policymakers and companies to find solutions for safer products and fair practices.

Our mission starts with you. We offer medical benefits that start on your first day as a CR employee that include behavioral health coverage, family planning and a generous 401K match. Learn more about how CR advocates on behalf of our employees.

Overview

CR’s Product Safety team analyzes data, identifies trends and risks, maintains data quality, and translates complex information into actionable recommendations that support Consumer Reports’ research, advocacy, and consumer-facing work.

As a Data Analyst (Consumer Product Safety) for the team, you will report to the Director, Product Safety within the Research and Insights division. This role supports the growing needs for data analysis, data quality validation, project tracking, and product safety investigations, as well as reviewing incident and testing data, identifying emerging safety risks, and supporting evidence-based decision-making across the organization.

You will work closely with the Product Safety team and partner cross-functionally with Content, Advocacy, Testing, and other business stakeholders to support investigations, reporting, consumer communications, and data-driven decision-making.

This is a Limited Duration position, expecting to end one year after start date. There is a possibility that the position will be extended. The position is designated as a hybrid for in-office collaboration and meetings as needed. This position is not eligible for sponsorship or relocation assistance.

How You'll Make An Impact

You will help Consumer Reports deliver trusted, evidence-based information by analyzing data, validating findings, identifying product safety risks, and supporting investigations, advocacy efforts, and consumer communications that empower consumers and improve marketplace safety. Areas of focus, include:

Performing basic analyses and creating visualizations while working under guidance.
Interpreting data analysis and developing actionable insights.
Conducting detailed reviews of laboratory reports, incident data, regulatory documents, and consumer complaints to identify emerging product safety risks and ensure data accuracy before findings support investigations, advocacy efforts, and consumer communications.
Applying strong attention to detail when evaluating testing results and project data, investigating anomalies, validating findings across multiple sources, and translating complex information into actionable insights that support evidence-based decision-making.
Creating and maintaining project trackers and workflow management tools to monitor investigation status, testing milestones, deliverables, and stakeholder engagement, ensuring timely execution, effective prioritization, and improved project visibility.

About You

You’ll Be Highly Rated If:

You have the education and/or experience. Bachelor's degree in a relevant field (Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, Computer Science) or appropriate knowledge and skills.
You value teamwork and collaboration. You can work with others to achieve shared goals while valuing differences, offering support and help and sharing knowledge and expertise to help the team.
You are committed to building and maintaining relationships in order to support and enhance personal, team and organizational work.
You have strong communication and organizational skills, plus attention to detail and the ability to manage time and tasks effectively.

You'll Be One Of Our Top Picks If

You are proficient when it comes to analytical software, such as Tableau, Power BI, Domo, Excel, SAS and R.

FAIR PAY AND A JUST WORKPLACE

At Consumer Reports, we are committed to fair, transparent pay and we strive to provide competitive, market-informed compensation.The target salary range for this position is $77,044.04 - $79,911.99 . It is anticipated that most qualified candidates will fall near the middle of this range. Compensation for the successful candidate will be informed by the candidate’s particular combination of knowledge, skills, competencies, and experience. We have three locations: Yonkers, NY, Washington, DC and Colchester, CT. We are registered to do business in and can only hire from the following states and federal district: Arizona, California, Connecticut, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Texas, Vermont, Virginia and Washington, DC.

jobs.description:
1155737_RR00115757 Job ID: 1155737_RR00115757

NYU Langone Health is a fully integrated health system that consistently achieves the best patient outcomes through a rigorous focus on quality that has resulted in some of the lowest mortality rates in the nation. Vizient Inc. has ranked NYU Langone the No. 1 comprehensive academic medical center in the country for three years in a row, and U.S. News & World Report recently placed nine of its clinical specialties among the top five in the nation. NYU Langone offers a comprehensive range of medical services with one high standard of care across 6 inpatient locations, its Perlmutter Cancer Center, and over 320 outpatient locations in the New York area and Florida. With $14.2 billion in revenue this year, the system also includes two tuition-free medical schools, in Manhattan and on Long Island, and a vast research enterprise with over $1 billion in active awards from the National Institutes of Health.

For more information, go to NYU Langone Health , and interact with us on LinkedIn , Glassdoor , Indeed , Facebook , Twitter , YouTube and Instagram .

Position Summary

We have an exciting opportunity to join our team as a Senior Analyst.

In this role, the successful candidate will provide data management and analytical support to the NYU Langone Health Faculty Group Practice (FGP) to assist in achieving the organization's financial and operational goals. As part of the fast-paced FGP Analytics team, the Senior Data Analyst will identify, query, and analyze data from a variety of sources to measure FGP performance, identify opportunities, and provide actionable insights to help drive NYU Langone Health business decisions. The Senior Data Analyst will have the creative vision to translate data into dynamic Tableau dashboards that will provide compelling analysis of operational and financial issues and provide self-service platforms to end-users. The position will act as liaison to the IT Data Warehouse team to assist in building and managing the FGPs internal data infrastructure and reporting systems.

To be effective, the Senior Data Analyst will have both a technical and analytical mindset to successfully collaborate with diverse teams and functional areas such as IT Data Warehouse and Analytics, EPIC EMR, Practice Operational Staff, and Corporate Management. The ideal candidate will be adept at using data to effectively communicate a business story to an audience with varying levels of technical knowledge.

The position will primarily focus on operational and financial projects for Patient Access, Revenue Cycle, and Practice Operations, Managed Care, and Clinically Integrated Network activity, as well assist on other projects as needed.

Job Responsibilities

The Senior Data Analyst will create data mining, predictive modeling, statistical reporting, and data analysis methodologies to identify trends and derive insights from large data sets.
The Senior Data Analyst will design and construct creative Tableau dashboards in collaboration with operational business units, who act as business customers and end-users of data and analyses. The dashboards are critical for providing timely data and insightful information to FGP Leadership and operational teams.
The Senior Data Analyst will work on Alteryx Designer and will develop workflows in Alteryx to query and create data sources for use in reporting and Tableau dashboards. Research new data sources and collaborate with technical partners to access and implement data feeds. Schedule and maintain the workflows to update data sources as needed for adapting business requirements. Troubleshoot dashboards, workflows, and underlying queries as needed.
The Senior Data Analyst will act as liaison to IT Data Warehouse, Reporting, and Dashboard teams. Work closely with the Data Architecture and Data Engineering teams to help create and optimize custom views and tables to enable reporting. Assist IT in enhancing the data warehouses including researching and validating tables and fields to support FGP business needs.
The Senior Data Analyst will analyze large datasets using appropriate systems to identify opportunities for FGP operational and revenue enhancement; assist in building new models and metrics; identify trends and develop work plans with management to address potential operations issues. Analyze large data sets in Excel to yield actionable findings.
The Senior Data Analyst will work with Revenue Cycle and Operational leaders and team members to assist with data gathering or interpretation. Ensure the integrity of Revenue Cycle and Operational reports and databases. Support the teams in dashboard build or operations performance analysis.
The Senior Data Analyst will act as data and dashboard evangelist to a wide audience of users, including analysts, practice operations staff, and senior leadership. Actively seek feedback from business users to improve insights of analyses and improve the functionality of dashboards.
The Senior Data Analyst will assist in mentoring junior members of NYU Langone Health analytics teams by sharing and teaching technical skills, prototyping analyses, and ensuring quality of results.

Minimum Qualifications

To qualify you must have:

Experience with SQL programming. (Writing SQL queries, stored procedure, functions, CTEs, subqueries, etc.).

Experience designing, developing, and building dashboards in Tableau or other data visualization tools.
Experience and comfort with any programing language (Python, SAS, etc.).
Familiarity with ETL processes for large datasets. Knowledge of Alteryx or similar ETL tools and cloud-based analytics platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, Tableau Prep, etc.).
Advanced knowledge of Excel; e.g. pivot tables, writing complex formulas.
Familiarity using generative AI to automate manual processes and enhance analytics.
Ability to communicate insights from data effectively across a cross-functional team environment.
Ability to work independently while also having a collaborative team mindset.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Strong interest in healthcare business and ability to consider higher-level business context.
Bachelors degree, preferably in a computer science, statistics, or healthcare related major; or the equivalent combination of experience and education.

Qualified candidates must be able to effectively communicate with all levels of the organization.

NYU Langone Health provides its staff with far more than just a place to work. Rather, we are an institution you can be proud of, an institution where you'll feel good about devoting your time and your talents.

At NYU Langone Health, we are committed to supporting our workforce and their loved ones with a comprehensive benefits and wellness package. Our offerings provide a robust support system for any stage of life, whether it's developing your career, starting a family, or saving for retirement. The support employees receive goes beyond a standard benefit offering, where employees have access to financial security benefits, a generous time-off program and employee resources groups for peer support. Additionally, all employees have access to our holistic employee wellness program, which focuses on seven key areas of well-being: physical, mental, nutritional, sleep, social, financial, and preventive care. The benefits and wellness package is designed to allow you to focus on what truly matters. Join us and experience the extensive resources and services designed to enhance your overall quality of life for you and your family.

The Nicotine Use Prevention and Control (NUPAC) Program is a program within the New Mexico Department of Health (NMDOH) that serves to provide education, prevention, and cessation services on nicotine and commercial tobacco use. NUPAC is housed within the the Population & Community Health Bureau (PCHB). PCHB advances the health of New Mexicans through a variety of programs and initiatives that address chronic disease prevention and management, oral health, increasing access to healthcare services through provider recruitment and retention and of clinical providers, supporting Community Health Workers/Community Health Representatives and by enhancing the delivery of clinical and behavioral services through School-Based Health Centers. The New Mexico Department of Health advances health equity by working with partners to promote health and well-being and improve health outcomes for all people in New Mexico. The Population and Community Health Bureau works to improve access to quality healthcare by providing collaborative leadership and resources to healthcare and community organizations.

Why does the job exist?

This position exists to engage and educate diverse communities to prevent nicotine use, especially among youth; build partnerships, promote cessation resources, and support equitable public health efforts through outreach, education and advocacy.

How does it get done?

Key responsibilities of this position include education and outreach to communities, community organizations, state agency programs, health councils, and other interested stakeholders across New Mexico; provide support to the northeast region of New Mexico and other regions, as needed; using evidence-based programs and initiatives to advance health equity; promoting and communicating NUPAC and NMDOH's initiatives; developing and negotiating scopes of work for contracts supporting efforts of the program; monitoring and providing oversight of contracted activities to ensure deliverables are completed within the timeline specified; providing technical assistance and support to contractors and stakeholders to enhance implementation of programs and services.

Who are the customers?

All New Mexico residents, community coalitions, health councils, program contractors and partners, public health professionals, health care professionals, community organizations, stakeholders, and other state agencies.

Ideal Candidate

Bachelor's degree in Health Education, Community Health, Public Health, or other health-related field of study. Master's degree a plus.
Experience in and knowledge of public health principles and approaches.
Moderate to advance writing skills for a variety of audiences. Experience in grant writing a plus.
Moderate to advanced public speaking skills; advance writing skills for a variety of audiences.
Experience working towards achieving health equity in priority populations, including tribal communities.

Minimum Qualification

Associate's Degree and two (2) years of experience program administration or project management in areas related to the purpose of the position. Any combination of education and/or direct experience in this occupation totaling four (4) years may substitute for the required education and experience.

Must possess and maintain a valid Driver's License.

Must possess and maintain a current Defensive Driving Course Certification from the State of New Mexico or must pass and receive Defensive Driving Course Certification as a condition of continued employment.

Work is usually performed in an office setting, with extensive exposure to Visual/Video Display Terminal (VDT), personal computer, tablet, printer, calculator, cellphone, copy machine, scanner, keyboard, and telephone usage. Occasional travel, working extra hours, working on weekends and holidays.

jobs.description:
Organization Overview:
The Goddard Riverside Community Center (Goddard Riverside) and the Stanley M. Isaacs Neighborhood Center (Isaacs Center) are well-established Manhattan community-based agencies that support services to approximately 30,000 New Yorkers. We are two of New York City’s leading human services organizations meeting the needs of children, youth and families, homeless individuals, and older adults through-out Manhattan and New York City. We work every day for a fair and just society where all people have the opportunity to make choices that lead to better lives for themselves and their families.

Program Description:
TOP Clubhouse is an evidence-based model that empowers individuals with a mental illness to focus on their own personal recovery goals through a holistic paradigm that integrates education, employment, and socialization. This unique model allows members to grow more confident in a range of tasks by working side-by-side with staff and other members. This model challenges the stigma around the client versus clinician relationship which is the more traditional model in many day programs. The clubhouse model considers staff and members as colleagues and every individual has unique talents and strengths that enhance our community.

Purpose of Position:
TOP Clubhouse is seeking a dynamic individual who will work with the Program Director to ensure quality and integrity of the program. This person must be innovative, creative, proactive, and detailed-oriented, with excellent organizational skills that will promote a positive work environment. The Program Coordinator is responsible for integrating services, staff support, and assuming overall leadership of TOP Clubhouse in the absence of the Program Director.

Schedule:
Monday through Friday 9:00 am to 5:00 pm. Some weekends and evenings required.

Salary Range:
$52,020 - $58,000

new_jobs.title: Cardiovascular Program Coordinator (Part Time, Day)
new_jobs.description:
Job Description

At NorthBay Health the Cardiovascular Program Coordinator is a RN who provides leadership across the continuum to promote excellence in cardiovascular patient care. This person has responsibility and accountability for operational design, program implementation, policy development, and population management of cardiovascular patient (Acute Coronary Syndrome (ACS), heart failure, and other cardiac or vascular diagnosis). The Cardiovascular Program Coordinator’s role is to promote the provision of effective, high quality patient care to cardiovascular patients across the continuum with a collegial and interdisciplinary focus.

The following responsibilities are included: coordination of fiscal and other resources, compliance with regulatory and professional standards, and fostering of interdisciplinary, collaborative relationships within the cardiology department and between other hospital departments. He/she will seek and provide educational programs for staff, physicians and community pertinent to the cardiovascular patient population served. Other responsibilities include coordination with the appropriate EMS and county agencies related to best practice responses and scene transports for patients experiencing cardiac emergencies, and coordination of quality and performance improvement initiatives.

The Cardiovascular Program Coordinator works collaboratively with the ED Chest Pain Center Medical Director, Medical Director-Cardiology, Medical Director-Interventional Lab, the Sr. Director – Critical Care & Cardiopulmonary Services, the clinical, and operational team to ensure that all patient care activities related to patients with cardiac-related conditions are standardized and based on current best practices. The Cardiovascular Program Coordinator ensures continuous departmental and organizational compliance with accreditation standards as established by the ACC and other applicable accrediting and regulatory bodies. The Cardiovascular Program Coordinator is responsible for data collection and analysis activities surrounding the cardiac and vascular programs, and other cardiovascular focused performance improvement activities.

Qualifications

Education:

Graduate from an accredited school of registered nursing.
Bachelor’s Degree in Nursing or related health care field is required.
Master’s Degree in Nursing or related health care field is required or required to be in progress. One of the degrees must be in nursing.

Licensure

Current California RN Licensure.
Certification by the American Nurse Credentialing Center preferred.
Current BLS and ACLS certification required.

Experience

Minimum 3 years of experience involved in the direct care of the cardiac patient in either the ED, Cath Lab, Cardiology, CCU or Telemetry.
Working knowledge of population management preferred.
Previous experience in an accredited chest pain center preferred.

Skills

Excellent oral and written communication skills required.
Must possess high professional standards.
Must be well organized, be able to set priorities, work independently and is an effective team member.
Word Processing skills required.
Statistical/data management skills preferred.

About Us

Interpersonal Skills and Values

Demonstrates NorthBay Health’s True North Values: Nurture Care, Own It, Respect Relationships, Build Trust, and Hardwire Excellence. These values guide behavior, accountability, teamwork, and commitment to high-quality patient care.

Why NorthBay Health

NorthBay Health is an independent, nonprofit health system serving the Napa, Solano, and Yolo County regions. We are expanding access to care across our communities through two acute-care hospitals, including a Level II Trauma Center and a Level III NICU maternity unit, along with a cancer center, urgent care locations, and a growing network of primary and specialty care clinics.

We provide advanced services in cardiovascular care, neuroscience, orthopedics, surgery, and outpatient specialties. NorthBay Health is nationally recognized for quality care, including Magnet with Distinction designation for nursing and multiple U.S. News and World Report high performing recognitions.

We are committed to being the trusted healthcare partner of choice and offer an environment where employees can grow, contribute meaningfully, and support the health of our communities.

NorthBay Health Benefits Options

NorthBay Health offers a comprehensive benefits package based on established eligibility requirements. Benefits may include medical, dental, and vision insurance, life, disability, and long-term care coverage, paid time off including vacation, sick leave, holidays, and bereavement, a 403(b) retirement plan with employer match, education reimbursement for eligible roles, professional development and training programs, Employee Assistance Program, wellness programs, recognition programs, shift differentials, and market-based compensation review and increases subject to approval and organizational performance.

Compensation Structure

NorthBay Health uses a structured compensation framework. Staff-level positions use a step-based system (Steps 1–5) based on years of directly related experience, with Step 5 representing 20 or more years of experience in the role. Manager level and above positions are paid a fixed annual base salary and are eligible for a variable incentive compensation plan. Physician compensation is structured based on specialty and role requirements.

Remote Work Disclosure

NorthBay Health is primarily an onsite organization due to the nature of healthcare. Some roles may allow hybrid or remote work based on business needs.

Remote work is not supported in Washington, Ohio, Wyoming, North Dakota, Puerto Rico, the U.S. Virgin Islands, or outside of the United States.

Notice to Recruitment Agencies

NorthBay Health utilizes a managed service provider (MSP) for agency partnerships and is not currently engaging external recruiting firms outside of established agreements. We do not accept unsolicited resumes or third-party candidate submissions. Please do not contact NorthBay Health employees, leaders, physicians, or hiring managers regarding recruitment or job postings.

More Information

Visit NorthBay Health Careers for recruitment FAQs and additional information.

new_jobs.description:
Job Specifications

Black Hills Energy is people powered and purpose driven. Our team uses the power of energy to improve life for over one million customers in 800+ communities across the West and Midwest. We seek talented, caring people who embody our core values and contribute to a culture of inclusion and growth. As an organization, we believe the best part of working on our team is our commitment to making tomorrow better than today—for our customers, communities and each other.

Position Summary

The GIS Technician's responsibilities include as-built interpretation, as-built posting to GIS, maintenance of the GIS database, mobile data collection support, communication with field operations and engineers, map production, occasional CAD work, and other miscellaneous projects as directed by the GIS Supervisor.

Pay Range:
$23.39 - $35.24 per hour (This posting includes the full pay range for this position. Pay is based on a number of factors and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, experience, and internal equity.)

Reporting Relationship:
GIS Supervisor

Location:
Fayetteville, Arkansas or Council Bluffs, Iowa

Essential Functions

Maintain the Esri Gas GIS database as well as quality check the data for inaccuracies.
Interpret gas transmission and distribution as-built drawings and post to GIS.
Understand Gas GIS operations and engineering processes as well as the gas assets and their functions.
Support the Digital As-built program. This means managing and support the system as well as training and support its usage in the field. Produce maps as required for Operations and Maintenance activities, system analysis, marketing, growth projects, etc.
Effectively communicate with staff at all levels regarding GIS.
Check accuracy of mapping system via field verification.
Diagnose software/hardware compatibility issues related to GIS products.
Use Esri tools such as ArcGIS (ArcMap & Pro) Desktop, desktop extensions (e.g. APR), ArcGIS Server, and Esri Portal in order to perform above job functions.
Provide feedback on above tools and processes in an effort to continuously improve gas as-builting accuracy, completeness, and efficiency.

Additional Responsibilities

Other duties as assigned.

What Is Required

High School Diploma or equivalent
Minimum of (1) one year of experience using Esri Desktop and Server/Portal based software

What Is Desired

Experience updating and maintaining a gas utility GIS is helpful.
Basic knowledge of Esri GIS systems and principles.
Basic knowledge of relational database management (RDBMS) concepts.
Basic knowledge of gas transmission and distribution systems.
Ability to think, work, and act independently as well as in a team environment to develop creative, analytical solutions to resolve complex problems.
Excellent customer service and interpersonal skills.
Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; ability to prepare and give presentations and trainings.
Strong organizational and time management skills.
Ability to prioritize and complete multiple tasks in a timely manner with minimal direction.
Proficiency in computer applications including Word, Excel, Power Point, Access, Outlook and various specialized software.

This description is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of responsibilities, duties, and requirements for employees in this position. Job descriptions may and do change periodically. Where positions are covered by a collective bargaining unit agreement, the terms and conditions of the agreement will apply.

Annual discretionary bonuses, 401(k) (6% company match and up to 9% company retirement contribution), tuition reimbursement, generous paid time off benefits, including paid holidays and parental leave, company paid life insurance and disability benefits (short and long term), critical illness, accident & group hospital insurance, pet insurance, an employee assistance program and well-being benefits, and competitive medical, dental and vision insurance.

will help help drive business value by leveraging data, analytics, and technology to optimize commercial and operational performance. new_jobs.title: Data Analyst II
new_jobs.description:
Position Summary...

What you'll do...Role summary:

The (USA) Data Analyst II plays a critical role in supporting business decision-making through comprehensive data analysis, quality management, and visualization. This position involves identifying relevant data sources, applying analytical techniques, and delivering actionable insights to drive project success. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to understand business context and communicate findings effectively. Proficiency in SQL, Python, and business intelligence tools is essential to support data-driven strategies and continuous improvement initiatives within a dynamic environment.

About the team:

The Transportation - Inbound Planning and Optimization team enhances the efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and service quality of inbound transportation operations. By leveraging advanced analytics, data-driven insights, and collaboration with operational partners, the team optimizes shipment planning, routing, and execution. Responsibilities include analyzing shipment patterns, ensuring data quality, conducting root cause analysis, and developing scalable solutions. Utilizing tools such as SQL, Python, and developing Agentic AI solutions, the team drives continuous improvement, process automation, and reliable transportation planning aligned with organizational goals. This team plays a vital role in translating business needs into actionable projects and system enhancements.

What you'll do:

Identify, extract, and validate data from multiple sources to support business requirements and process optimization.
Perform initial and ongoing data quality checks, ensuring data is fit for purpose and meets business needs.
Apply data strategy principles, including data governance, accessibility, and scalability, to routine and complex business problems.
Conduct exploratory data analysis using SQL, Python, and statistical techniques to uncover trends, outliers, and opportunities for improvement.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business context, develop business cases, and translate requirements into actionable projects.
Demonstrate strong project management skills, stakeholder management, and a continuous improvement mindset.

What you'll bring:

Proficiency in SQL, Python, and data visualization tools such as Tableau to analyze and present complex data effectively.
Strong understanding of data quality management, including profiling, cleansing, and validation techniques.
Experience in exploratory data analysis to identify trends, correlations, and outliers.

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst (Consumer Product Safety), Limited Duration
new_jobs.description:
Who We Are

Consumer Reports is an independent, nonprofit organization dedicated to a fair and just marketplace for all. CR is known for our rigorous testing and trusted ratings on thousands of products and services. We report extensively on consumer trends and challenges, and survey millions of people in the U.S. each year. We leverage our evidence-based approach to advocate for consumer rights, working with policymakers and companies to find solutions for safer products and fair practices.

Our mission starts with you. We offer medical benefits that start on your first day as a CR employee that include behavioral health coverage, family planning and a generous 401K match. Learn more about how CR advocates on behalf of our employees.

Overview

CR’s Product Safety team analyzes data, identifies trends and risks, maintains data quality, and translates complex information into actionable recommendations that support Consumer Reports’ research, advocacy, and consumer-facing work.

As a Data Analyst (Consumer Product Safety) for the team, you will report to the Director, Product Safety within the Research and Insights division. This role supports the growing needs for data analysis, data quality validation, project tracking, and product safety investigations, as well as reviewing incident and testing data, identifying emerging safety risks, and supporting evidence-based decision-making across the organization.

You will work closely with the Product Safety team and partner cross-functionally with Content, Advocacy, Testing, and other business stakeholders to support investigations, reporting, consumer communications, and data-driven decision-making.

This is a Limited Duration position, expecting to end one year after start date. There is a possibility that the position will be extended. The position is designated as a hybrid for in-office collaboration and meetings as needed. This position is not eligible for sponsorship or relocation assistance.

How You'll Make An Impact

You will help Consumer Reports deliver trusted, evidence-based information by analyzing data, validating findings, identifying product safety risks, and supporting investigations, advocacy efforts, and consumer communications that empower consumers and improve marketplace safety. Areas of focus, include:

Performing basic analyses and creating visualizations while working under guidance.
Interpreting data analysis and developing actionable insights.
Conducting detailed reviews of laboratory reports, incident data, regulatory documents, and consumer complaints to identify emerging product safety risks and ensure data accuracy before findings support investigations, advocacy efforts, and consumer communications.
Applying strong attention to detail when evaluating testing results and project data, investigating anomalies, validating findings across multiple sources, and translating complex information into actionable insights that support evidence-based decision-making.
Creating and maintaining project trackers and workflow management tools to monitor investigation status, testing milestones, deliverables, and stakeholder engagement, ensuring timely execution, effective prioritization, and improved project visibility.

About You

You’ll Be Highly Rated If:

You have the education and/or experience. Bachelor's degree in a relevant field (Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, Computer Science) or appropriate knowledge and skills.
You value teamwork and collaboration. You can work with others to achieve shared goals while valuing differences, offering support and help and sharing knowledge and expertise to help the team.
You are committed to building and maintaining relationships in order to support and enhance personal, team and organizational work.
You have strong communication and organizational skills, plus attention to detail and the ability to manage time and tasks effectively.

You'll Be One Of Our Top Picks If

You are proficient when it comes to analytical software, such as Tableau, Power BI, Domo, Excel, SAS and R.

FAIR PAY AND A JUST WORKPLACE

At Consumer Reports, we are committed to fair, transparent pay and we strive to provide competitive, market-informed compensation.The target salary range for this position is $77,044.04 - $79,911.99 . It is anticipated that most qualified candidates will fall near the middle of this range. Compensation for the successful candidate will be informed by the candidate’s particular combination of knowledge, skills, competencies, and experience. We have three locations: Yonkers, NY, Washington, DC and Colchester, CT. We are registered to do business in and can only hire from the following states and federal district: Arizona, California, Connecticut, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Texas, Vermont, Virginia and Washington, DC.

In this role, the successful candidate will provide data management and analytical support to the NYU Langone Health Faculty Group Practice (FGP) to assist in achieving the organization's financial and operational goals. As part of the fast-paced FGP Analytics team, the Senior Data Analyst will identify, query, and analyze data from a variety of sources to measure FGP performance, identify opportunities, and provide actionable insights to help drive NYU Langone Health business decisions. The Senior Data Analyst will have the creative vision to translate data into dynamic Tableau dashboards that will provide compelling analysis of operational and financial issues and provide self-service platforms to end-users. The position will act as liaison to the IT Data Warehouse team to assist in building and managing the FGPs internal data infrastructure and reporting systems.

To be effective, the Senior Data Analyst will have both a technical and analytical mindset to successfully collaborate with diverse teams and functional areas such as IT Data Warehouse and Analytics, EPIC EMR, Practice Operational Staff, and Corporate Management. The ideal candidate will be adept at using data to effectively communicate a business story to an audience with varying levels of technical knowledge.

The position will primarily focus on operational and financial projects for Patient Access, Revenue Cycle, and Practice Operations, Managed Care, and Clinically Integrated Network activity, as well assist on other projects as needed.

Job Responsibilities

The Senior Data Analyst will create data mining, predictive modeling, statistical reporting, and data analysis methodologies to identify trends and derive insights from large data sets.
The Senior Data Analyst will design and construct creative Tableau dashboards in collaboration with operational business units, who act as business customers and end-users of data and analyses. The dashboards are critical for providing timely data and insightful information to FGP Leadership and operational teams.
The Senior Data Analyst will work on Alteryx Designer and will develop workflows in Alteryx to query and create data sources for use in reporting and Tableau dashboards. Research new data sources and collaborate with technical partners to access and implement data feeds. Schedule and maintain the workflows to update data sources as needed for adapting business requirements. Troubleshoot dashboards, workflows, and underlying queries as needed.
The Senior Data Analyst will act as liaison to IT Data Warehouse, Reporting, and Dashboard teams. Work closely with the Data Architecture and Data Engineering teams to help create and optimize custom views and tables to enable reporting. Assist IT in enhancing the data warehouses including researching and validating tables and fields to support FGP business needs.
The Senior Data Analyst will analyze large datasets using appropriate systems to identify opportunities for FGP operational and revenue enhancement; assist in building new models and metrics; identify trends and develop work plans with management to address potential operations issues. Analyze large data sets in Excel to yield actionable findings.
The Senior Data Analyst will work with Revenue Cycle and Operational leaders and team members to assist with data gathering or interpretation. Ensure the integrity of Revenue Cycle and Operational reports and databases. Support the teams in dashboard build or operations performance analysis.
The Senior Data Analyst will act as data and dashboard evangelist to a wide audience of users, including analysts, practice operations staff, and senior leadership. Actively seek feedback from business users to improve insights of analyses and improve the functionality of dashboards.
The Senior Data Analyst will assist in mentoring junior members of NYU Langone Health analytics teams by sharing and teaching technical skills, prototyping analyses, and ensuring quality of results.

Minimum Qualifications

To qualify you must have:

Experience with SQL programming. (Writing SQL queries, stored procedure, functions, CTEs, subqueries, etc.).

Experience designing, developing, and building dashboards in Tableau or other data visualization tools.
Experience and comfort with any programing language (Python, SAS, etc.).
Familiarity with ETL processes for large datasets. Knowledge of Alteryx or similar ETL tools and cloud-based analytics platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, Tableau Prep, etc.).
Advanced knowledge of Excel; e.g. pivot tables, writing complex formulas.
Familiarity using generative AI to automate manual processes and enhance analytics.
Ability to communicate insights from data effectively across a cross-functional team environment.
Ability to work independently while also having a collaborative team mindset.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Strong interest in healthcare business and ability to consider higher-level business context.
Bachelors degree, preferably in a computer science, statistics, or healthcare related major; or the equivalent combination of experience and education.

Qualified candidates must be able to effectively communicate with all levels of the organization.

Posting Details

THIS POSTING WILL BE USED FOR ONGOING RECRUITMENT AND MAY CLOSE AT ANY TIME. APPLICANT LISTS MAY BE SCREENED MORE THAN ONCE.

The Nicotine Use Prevention and Control (NUPAC) Program is a program within the New Mexico Department of Health (NMDOH) that serves to provide education, prevention, and cessation services on nicotine and commercial tobacco use. NUPAC is housed within the the Population & Community Health Bureau (PCHB). PCHB advances the health of New Mexicans through a variety of programs and initiatives that address chronic disease prevention and management, oral health, increasing access to healthcare services through provider recruitment and retention and of clinical providers, supporting Community Health Workers/Community Health Representatives and by enhancing the delivery of clinical and behavioral services through School-Based Health Centers. The New Mexico Department of Health advances health equity by working with partners to promote health and well-being and improve health outcomes for all people in New Mexico. The Population and Community Health Bureau works to improve access to quality healthcare by providing collaborative leadership and resources to healthcare and community organizations.

Why does the job exist?

This position exists to engage and educate diverse communities to prevent nicotine use, especially among youth; build partnerships, promote cessation resources, and support equitable public health efforts through outreach, education and advocacy.

How does it get done?

Key responsibilities of this position include education and outreach to communities, community organizations, state agency programs, health councils, and other interested stakeholders across New Mexico; provide support to the northeast region of New Mexico and other regions, as needed; using evidence-based programs and initiatives to advance health equity; promoting and communicating NUPAC and NMDOH's initiatives; developing and negotiating scopes of work for contracts supporting efforts of the program; monitoring and providing oversight of contracted activities to ensure deliverables are completed within the timeline specified; providing technical assistance and support to contractors and stakeholders to enhance implementation of programs and services.

Who are the customers?

All New Mexico residents, community coalitions, health councils, program contractors and partners, public health professionals, health care professionals, community organizations, stakeholders, and other state agencies.

Ideal Candidate

Bachelor's degree in Health Education, Community Health, Public Health, or other health-related field of study. Master's degree a plus.
Experience in and knowledge of public health principles and approaches.
Moderate to advance writing skills for a variety of audiences. Experience in grant writing a plus.
Moderate to advanced public speaking skills; advance writing skills for a variety of audiences.
Experience working towards achieving health equity in priority populations, including tribal communities.

Minimum Qualification

Associate's Degree and two (2) years of experience program administration or project management in areas related to the purpose of the position. Any combination of education and/or direct experience in this occupation totaling four (4) years may substitute for the required education and experience.

Employment Requirements

Must possess and maintain a valid Driver's License.

Must possess and maintain a current Defensive Driving Course Certification from the State of New Mexico or must pass and receive Defensive Driving Course Certification as a condition of continued employment.

Working Conditions

Work is usually performed in an office setting, with extensive exposure to Visual/Video Display Terminal (VDT), personal computer, tablet, printer, calculator, cellphone, copy machine, scanner, keyboard, and telephone usage. Occasional travel, working extra hours, working on weekends and holidays.

new_jobs.title: Program Coordinator (TOP Clubhouse)
new_jobs.description:
Organization Overview:
The Goddard Riverside Community Center (Goddard Riverside) and the Stanley M. Isaacs Neighborhood Center (Isaacs Center) are well-established Manhattan community-based agencies that support services to approximately 30,000 New Yorkers. We are two of New York City’s leading human services organizations meeting the needs of children, youth and families, homeless individuals, and older adults through-out Manhattan and New York City. We work every day for a fair and just society where all people have the opportunity to make choices that lead to better lives for themselves and their families.

Program Description:
TOP Clubhouse is an evidence-based model that empowers individuals with a mental illness to focus on their own personal recovery goals through a holistic paradigm that integrates education, employment, and socialization. This unique model allows members to grow more confident in a range of tasks by working side-by-side with staff and other members. This model challenges the stigma around the client versus clinician relationship which is the more traditional model in many day programs. The clubhouse model considers staff and members as colleagues and every individual has unique talents and strengths that enhance our community.

Purpose of Position:
TOP Clubhouse is seeking a dynamic individual who will work with the Program Director to ensure quality and integrity of the program. This person must be innovative, creative, proactive, and detailed-oriented, with excellent organizational skills that will promote a positive work environment. The Program Coordinator is responsible for integrating services, staff support, and assuming overall leadership of TOP Clubhouse in the absence of the Program Director.

Schedule:
Monday through Friday 9:00 am to 5:00 pm. Some weekends and evenings required.

Salary Range:
$52,020 - $58,000

Candidates must have the required qualifications to be considered for the position. The s alary offer will be based on the candidate’s number of years experience in the exact or comparable position and additional preferred qualifications will be taken into account.
